ddls:
CREATE TABLE "system_calls" (
  "call_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system call record */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the system call was made */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server on which the system call was made */,
  "accept" REAL /* Metric for the accept system call */,
  "address" REAL /* Metric for the address system call */,
  "bind" REAL /* Metric for the bind system call */,
  "brk" REAL /* Metric for the brk system call */,
  "clone" REAL /* Metric for the clone system call */,
  "close" REAL /* Metric for the close system call */,
  "connect" REAL /* Metric for the connect system call */,
  "dup" REAL /* Metric for the dup system call */,
  "exit" REAL /* Metric for the exit system call */,
  "fcntl" REAL /* Metric for the fcntl system call */,
  "futex" REAL /* Metric for the futex system call */,
  "getpid" INTEGER /* PID of the process making the system call */,
  "getsockopt" REAL /* Metric for the getsockopt system call */,
  "ioctl" REAL /* Metric for the ioctl system call */,
  "lseek" REAL /* Metric for the lseek system call */,
  "madvise" REAL /* Metric for the madvise system call */,
  "mmap" REAL /* Metric for the mmap system call */,
  "mprotect" REAL /* Metric for the mprotect system call */,
  "munmap" REAL /* Metric for the munmap system call */,
  "newfstat" REAL /* Metric for the newfstat system call */,
  "newlstat" REAL /* Metric for the newlstat system call */,
  "newstat" REAL /* Metric for the newstat system call */,
  "open" REAL /* Metric for the open system call */,
  "poll" REAL /* Metric for the poll system call */,
  "read" REAL /* Metric for the read system call */,
  "readv" REAL /* Metric for the readv system call */,
  "recvfrom" REAL /* Metric for the recvfrom system call */,
  "select" REAL /* Metric for the select system call */,
  "sendto" REAL /* Metric for the sendto system call */,
  "setsockopt" REAL /* Metric for the setsockopt system call */,
  "shutdown" REAL /* Metric for the shutdown system call */,
  "sigprocmask" REAL /* Metric for the sigprocmask system call */,
  "socket" REAL /* Metric for the socket system call */,
  "writev" REAL /* Metric for the writev system call */,
  "application_name" TEXT /* Name of the application making the system call */,
  "system_call_type" TEXT /* Type of system call (e.g., read, write, connect) */,
  "request_size" INTEGER /* Size of the request in bytes */,
  "response_size" INTEGER /* Size of the response in bytes */,
  "response_time" REAL /* Response time of the system call in milliseconds */,
  "status_code" INTEGER /* Status code of the system call (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("call_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_calls_system_call_type FOREIGN KEY ("system_call_type") REFERENCES system_call_types ("system_call_type"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_calls_application_name FOREIGN KEY ("application_name") REFERENCES applications ("application_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "cpu_count" INTEGER /* Number of CPUs in the server */,
  "memory_size" INTEGER /* Size of the server's memory in GB */,
  "disk_space" INTEGER /* Size of the server's disk space in GB */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the user's password */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server being accessed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view, download) */,
  "data_requested" TEXT /* Description of the data requested by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server associated with the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* The message detailing the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was generated */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., system call, resource usage) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server to which the threshold applies */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric being monitored */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Value that defines the alert threshold */,
  "operator" TEXT /* Operator for the threshold (e.g., greater than, less than) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "application_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_call_types" (
  "system_call_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system call type */,
  "system_call_type" TEXT /* Name of the system call type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the system call type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_call_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_call_metrics" (
  "system_call_metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system call metric */,
  "system_call_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system call type to which the metric applies */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_call_metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_call_results" (
  "system_call_result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system call result */,
  "call_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system call to which the result applies */,
  "status_code" INTEGER /* Status code of the system call */,
  "response_time" REAL /* Response time of the system call in milliseconds */,
  "response_size" INTEGER /* Size of the response in bytes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_call_result_id")
)
